graph.topic: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments .
graph.researcher_bg: An HCI researcher specializing in Educational Technology (EdTech) , focused on optimizing complex grading workflows and feedback quality
graph.expert_bg: A seasoned university instructor with extensive experience in evaluating lengthy project reports

q_01__shared_root.label: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments .
q_01__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments .

q_01__exp_L1_00.label: Expert Grading Workflow & Intuition
q_01__exp_L1_00.description: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments.

q_01__exp_L2_00_00.label: Workflow Logistics & Pacing
q_01__exp_L2_00_00.description: Managing the physical and temporal mechanics of getting through a stack of reports without burning out.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: The Two-Pass Sweep
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: I don't grade linearly on the first try. I skim the summary and TOC first, then do a quick pass for structure before diving deep for content.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Annotation Timing
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: I have to decide whether to mark errors in the margins as I read or wait until I've finished the section to avoid breaking my flow.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_02.label: Flagging Format Violations Early
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_02.description: If the margins are wrong or the bibliography is missing, I stop right there. No point grading content if they didn't follow the basic rules of the game.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_03.label: The 'Five-Paper Limit' Rule
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_03.description: I force myself to stop after five reports. My feedback quality tanks if I try to power through ten in a row, so I cap the batch size.

q_01__exp_L2_00_01.label: Grading Consistency & Calibration
q_01__exp_L2_00_01.description: Ensuring that a student's grade depends on their work, not on where they fell in the grading queue.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Re-anchoring Mid-Batch
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: By paper fifteen, I realize I've gotten too easy. I have to pause and re-read the first paper to remember where I started and reset my strictness.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: The 'Polish vs. Substance' Trade-off
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: A pretty report tricks me into thinking the ideas are better. I have to consciously ignore the formatting to find the gold underneath the slick presentation.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_02.label: Routine Rubric Cross-Check
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_02.description: I'm constantly pausing to match specific paragraphs against rubric criteria while reading the technical sections.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_03.label: When the Rubric Doesn't Fit
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_03.description: Sometimes a student does something brilliant that isn't on the checklist. I have to decide in the moment if I reward the innovation or stick to the script.

q_01__exp_L2_00_02.label: Cognitive Load & Integrity
q_01__exp_L2_00_02.description: Monitoring my own mental state and detecting when student work doesn't feel authentic.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: Recognizing the 'Glaze Over' Moment
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: There's a specific feeling where the words stop making sense. I know I've hit the wall and need to walk away before I miss something obvious.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_01.label: The 'Voice Shift' Detection
q_01__exp_leaf_00_02_01.description: The writing style suddenly jumps from sophomore level to PhD thesis in paragraph three. I can't prove it, but my spidey senses tingle.

q_01__shared_root.label: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments .
q_01__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments .

q_01__res_L1_00.label: Interaction Mechanics & Toolchain
q_01__res_L1_00.description: Examining the specific software tools, input methods, and navigation patterns used during the grading process.

q_01__res_L2_00_00.label: Tool Interoperability & Input
q_01__res_L2_00_00.description: Understanding how educators move between different software environments and input grades or comments.

q_01__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Identify context switching frequency between LMS, PDF annotators, and gradebooks
q_01__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Determine how often the educator must alt-tab or switch windows to complete a single student's grade, highlighting potential friction points in tool integration.

q_01__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Determine if grading input is inline or post-reading
q_01__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Explore whether educators assign scores and write feedback while reading the document simultaneously or as a separate action after reading.

q_01__res_L2_00_01.label: Navigation Strategies
q_01__res_L2_00_01.description: Analyzing how educators move through the queue of submissions and within individual documents.

q_01__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Map inter-student navigation patterns
q_01__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Understand if educators grade submissions linearly (next button) or access them randomly from a list, affecting how mental context is maintained.

q_01__res_leaf_00_01_01.label: Analyze intra-document foraging behaviors
q_01__res_leaf_00_01_01.description: Investigate how educators locate specific sections within a long report (e.g., scrolling, searching, bookmarks) to find evidence for grading.

q_01__res_L1_01.label: Information Artifacts & Data Flow
q_01__res_L1_01.description: Investigating how grading criteria, rubrics, and feedback are managed, referenced, and stored during the workflow.

q_01__res_L2_01_00.label: Rubric Engagement
q_01__res_L2_01_00.description: Understanding the physical or digital relationship between the grading rubric and the student submission.

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Assess rubric visibility during grading actions
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Determine if the rubric is always visible, toggled on/off, or printed separately, impacting working memory load.

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Identify criteria-to-evidence mapping methods
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Explore how educators link specific text in a report to specific rubric criteria (e.g., highlighting, side notes, mental mapping).

q_01__res_L2_01_01.label: Feedback Composition
q_01__res_L2_01_01.description: Examining where and how feedback text is generated and attached to the final grade.

q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Locate feedback storage relative to the submission
q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Identify if feedback is written directly on the PDF, in a separate LMS text box, or in an external document before copying.

q_01__res_leaf_01_01_01.label: Determine feedback drafting vs. finalizing steps
q_01__res_leaf_01_01_01.description: Understand if feedback is written in a single pass or drafted elsewhere and edited before submission to the student.

q_01__res_L1_02.label: Progress Tracking & State Management
q_01__res_L1_02.description: Exploring how educators monitor their progress through the grading queue and manage the state of unfinished work.

q_01__res_L2_02_00.label: Workflow Visibility
q_01__res_L2_02_00.description: Understanding how educators know what is done, what is pending, and when a task is complete.

q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.label: Identify mechanisms for tracking completion status
q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.description: Explore how educators mark a submission as 'graded' (e.g., checkbox, color change, moving to a folder) to prevent double grading.

q_01__res_leaf_02_00_01.label: Define task completion criteria per student
q_01__res_leaf_02_00_01.description: Determine what specific action signals the end of the grading workflow for a single assignment (e.g., clicking submit, saving file).

assistance_payload.validate_focus: Your focus on capping batch sizes to preserve feedback quality is highly applicable for managing cognitive fatigue. However, we might encounter hidden friction that undermines even those limited sessions.

pivot.limitation: Even within a small batch, constant window switching between disjointed applications drains cognitive energy faster than the grading itself.
pivot.research_goal: Identifying context switching frequency between LMS, PDF annotators, and gradebooks.
pivot.compelling_reason: Solving the identification of context switching frequency helps us overcome this because it ensures your mental endurance is spent on student feedback rather than navigation, effectively extending your high-quality grading window.
pivot.coarse_scenario: Imagine finishing your batch of five reports feeling mentally fresh because the process flowed together seamlessly. You leave work on time without the usual digital exhaustion, ready to engage with students tomorrow rather than recovering from screen fatigue.

conflict.researcher_branch: Define task completion criteria per student

assistance_payload.validate_focus: Your focus on re-anchoring mid-batch is highly applicable for maintaining fairness, but we might encounter consistency gaps if this process relies entirely on manual memory and fatigue-prone pauses.

pivot.limitation: Manual re-anchoring is cognitively taxing and easy to skip when fatigued, leading to hidden grading drift that undermines your quality assurance.
pivot.research_goal: Defining workflow states that include these quality assurance interruptions as formal completion criteria rather than just per-student task endings.
pivot.compelling_reason: Solving this helps us overcome the drift because it transforms your intuitive quality checks into a sustainable system that protects your grading standards without increasing your mental load.
pivot.coarse_scenario: Imagine a day where the system recognizes when you've graded ten papers and gently prompts you to review a past example before continuing, ensuring your standards stay locked in without you having to stop and search for old files yourself. This flow keeps your consistency automatic rather than effortful, letting you focus on the student work instead of monitoring your own strictness.

q_02__exp_L1_00.label: Expert Grading Challenges
q_02__exp_L1_00.description: The cognitive, technical, and systemic hurdles instructors face when evaluating lengthy project reports.

q_02__exp_L2_00_00.label: Cognitive Load & Grading Drift
q_02__exp_L2_00_00.description: Managing mental stamina and maintaining consistent standards across a large volume of submissions.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Grading Fatigue & Focus
q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: Keeping my head in the game when grading 20+ reports. After a while, the reports start to blur together and I hit a mental wall.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Consistency Drift
q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: Standards slip or overcorrect as I move through the stack. By the fifth report I'm softening, by the fifteenth I'm second-guessing if I'm being too harsh or lenient.

q_02__exp_L2_00_01.label: Content vs. Presentation
q_02__exp_L2_00_01.description: Distinguishing between the quality of the actual work and the surface-level features that can distract or deceive.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Polish vs. Substance Trade-off
q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: A slick layout or pretty report tricks me into thinking the ideas are better. It's hard to ignore the formatting to find the gold underneath.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Format Distraction
q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: Chasing style violations like margins or citations becomes a red flag that distracts me from the actual content. It's a tug-of-war between the rubric and the work.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_02.label: Innovation vs. Error Discrimination
q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_02.description: Sometimes a student tries something wild that isn't in the textbook. It looks wrong at first glance, but I have to figure out if they're onto something new or just broke the model.

q_02__exp_L2_00_02.label: Integrity & Intuition
q_02__exp_L2_00_02.description: Relying on expert instinct to detect authenticity issues when evidence is ambiguous or tools are unreliable.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: Authorship Intuition
q_02__exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: The writing style suddenly jumps from sophomore level to PhD thesis. I can't prove it, but my gut screams something's off or it feels soulless.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_02_01.label: Time & Volume Pressure
q_02__exp_leaf_00_02_01.description: When grades are due or the stack is huge, I know I'm skimming more than I should. I hate that the clock dictates my rigor more than the student's work does.

q_02__shared_root.label: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments .

q_02__res_L1_00.label: Technical & Environmental Friction
q_02__res_L1_00.description: Investigating external barriers and tool limitations that impede the grading process beyond internal workflow choices.

q_02__res_L2_00_00.label: Multimodal Artifact Integration
q_02__res_L2_00_00.description: Challenges arising from assessing non-text components linked to the written report.

q_02__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Grading Linked Artifacts
q_02__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Understand the difficulty of verifying code repositories or data sets that are referenced but not embedded in the report.

q_02__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Dynamic Content Verification
q_02__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Explore the friction involved in assessing embedded videos or demos within static grading windows.

q_02__res_L2_00_01.label: Platform Interoperability
q_02__res_L2_00_01.description: Issues related to moving between different software environments during assessment.

q_02__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: LMS vs. External Tool Friction
q_02__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Identify cognitive costs associated with switching between the Learning Management System gradebook and local review tools.

q_02__res_L1_01.label: Evaluative Ambiguity & Conflict
q_02__res_L1_01.description: Exploring the inherent difficulties in judging subjective work and managing the social consequences of grading.

q_02__res_L2_01_00.label: Comparative Fairness
q_02__res_L2_01_00.description: Challenges in ensuring equity when student projects vary widely in topic or execution style.

q_02__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Cross-Topic Normalization
q_02__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Investigate the struggle to fairly compare fundamentally different project approaches against a single rubric.

q_02__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Individual Contribution Isolation
q_02__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Understand the difficulty of discerning individual effort within group-written reports.

q_02__res_L2_01_01.label: Conflict Anticipation
q_02__res_L2_01_01.description: The mental load associated with predicting and preventing student disputes over grades.

q_02__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Preemptive Justification Load
q_02__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Explore the extra effort spent writing evidence to defend against potential grade appeals.

q_02__res_L1_02.label: Feedback Efficacy & ROI
q_02__res_L1_02.description: Examining the perceived value of feedback efforts and the uncertainty of student engagement.

q_02__res_L2_02_00.label: Consumption Uncertainty
q_02__res_L2_02_00.description: Challenges related to not knowing if or how students engage with provided feedback.

q_02__res_leaf_02_00_00.label: Feedback Loop Closure
q_02__res_leaf_02_00_00.description: Identify frustration points when detailed feedback appears ignored by students (lack of closure).

q_02__res_leaf_02_00_01.label: Receptiveness Tailoring
q_02__res_leaf_02_00_01.description: Understand the difficulty of adjusting feedback depth based on perceived student receptiveness.

q_02__res_L2_02_01.label: Actionability Balance
q_02__res_L2_02_01.description: The trade-off between explaining the current grade versus guiding future improvement.

q_02__res_leaf_02_01_00.label: Justification vs. Instruction
q_02__res_leaf_02_01_00.description: Explore the challenge of writing feedback that is actionable for future projects rather than justifying the current score.

q_03__exp_L1_00.label: Expert Grading Challenges
q_03__exp_L1_00.description: The cognitive, emotional, and systemic hurdles instructors face when evaluating lengthy project reports.

q_03__exp_L2_00_00.label: Entering the Workflow
q_03__exp_L2_00_00.description: The mental barriers, setup rituals, and calibration effort needed to start grading large volumes.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: The Pre-Grading Dread
q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: It's that heavy feeling in your chest before you even open the first PDF. You know it's going to take six hours, and just staring at the folder icon feels like lifting weights.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: The Calibration Drag
q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: The first batch is the heaviest mentally. I'm not just grading; I'm defining what 'good' looks like for this stack, and that takes double the brainpower.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_02.label: The Personal Stop Signal
q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_02.description: I know I'm done for the night not by the clock, but when I start reading the same sentence twice and not remembering it. That's my gut telling me my brain is full.

q_03__exp_L2_00_01.label: Sustaining Empathy
q_03__exp_L2_00_01.description: Maintaining a human connection to the student behind the work despite repetitive evaluation.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Emotional Carry-Over
q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: If I just graded a really sloppy, disrespectful report, I have to take a breath before opening the next one so I don't unfairly punish the next student for the previous one's mess.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Student Dehumanization
q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: By report number fifteen, they stop feeling like individual learners and start feeling like tickets in a queue. I have to consciously remind myself there's a person on the other end.

q_03__exp_L2_00_02.label: The Mid-Stack Slump
q_03__exp_L2_00_02.description: The phase in the middle of the pile where novelty wears off, memory fades, and decision-making becomes compromised.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: The Voice Blend
q_03__exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: Around the middle of the stack, all the student voices start sounding like me. I stop hearing their ideas and just start hearing my own expectations reflected back.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_02_01.label: Nuance Blindness
q_03__exp_leaf_00_02_01.description: My brain starts auto-completing their arguments. I miss a clever twist because I've seen the same wrong answer ten times already and I assume this is another one.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_02_02.label: Consistency Anxiety
q_03__exp_leaf_00_02_02.description: In the thick of it, I start doubting my own rubric. Am I being too harsh compared to report #5? The standard feels slippery and I can't trust my own judgment anymore.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_02_03.label: Repetition Amnesia
q_03__exp_leaf_00_02_03.description: I can't remember if I've already graded that specific issue or not. It's like my brain's stuck in a loop, seeing the same mistakes over and over without tracking them.

q_03__exp_L2_00_03.label: The Final Stretch Compromise
q_03__exp_L2_00_03.description: The end of the grading session where the desire to finish conflicts with the duty to evaluate thoroughly.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_03_00.label: Impatience Penalties
q_03__exp_leaf_00_03_00.description: I catch myself getting annoyed at minor formatting errors in the last few reports simply because I want to be done. I have to check myself to make sure I'm not penalizing them for my tiredness.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_03_01.label: The Final Skim Guilt
q_03__exp_leaf_00_03_01.description: I know I skimmed the last three submissions more than the first three. It keeps me up at night wondering if I missed a breakthrough idea or was too lenient/strict because I was just looking for reasons to close the file.

assistance_payload.validate_focus: Your focus on using a personal stop signal to prevent cognitive overload is highly applicable. It serves as a critical workflow safeguard for maintaining grading consistency.

pivot.limitation: But we might encounter hidden emotional guilt that undermines your willingness to use that signal effectively when pressure mounts.

q_04__exp_L1_00.label: Expert Grading Intuition
q_04__exp_L1_00.description: Identifying the objective cues and pattern recognition signals that underlie an expert's 'gut feeling' when distinguishing high-quality from low-quality project reports.

q_04__exp_L2_00_00.label: Narrative Cohesion & Logic
q_04__exp_L2_00_00.description: How well the report tells a unified story from start to finish, signaling whether the student truly understands their own project.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Argument Alignment
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: I check if the specific question asked in the introduction is the exact one answered in the conclusion. If they pivot to something easier halfway through, that's an instant C.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Reading Friction
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: It's that feeling when I have to re-read a sentence twice or when ideas jump without transition. An A report connects facts into a story; a C report just lists them. It flows like water vs wading through mud.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_02.label: Argument Scanability
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_02.description: When I skim, does the core argument jump out at me? If I have to hunt for the main point, that's a problem. A reports make the key findings obvious immediately.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_03.label: Figure Independence
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_03.description: I cover the body text and read only the captions. If I can't tell what happened in the project just from the images and captions, the data presentation is weak.

q_04__exp_L2_00_01.label: Technical Execution & Precision
q_04__exp_L2_00_01.description: The micro-level details that show whether the student did the work themselves or just slapped words together.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Verb Precision
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: I scan for weak verbs like 'looked at' or 'did' versus strong ones like 'calculated' or 'simulated'. Weak verbs usually mean weak thinking.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Tone Stability
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: I look for confidence—does it sound like someone who owns the data? Red flags are trying too hard to sound smart or being flat and unengaged. Guessing sounds different from performing.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_02.label: Citation Weaving
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_02.description: Are citations parked at the end of a paragraph like an afterthought, or are they integrated into the sentence to support a specific claim?

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_03.label: Rubric Integration
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_03.description: I check if they addressed the rubric requirements naturally within the flow, or if it feels like they just checked boxes. Box-checking feels mechanical; integration feels thoughtful.

q_04__exp_L2_00_02.label: Effort & Professionalism Signals
q_04__exp_L2_00_02.description: Meta-cues that indicate how much care and time were invested in the final product.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: Space Allocation
q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: I glance at the page ratios. If the methodology is two pages but the analysis is half a page, they spent all their time setting up and none on thinking.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_01.label: Formatting Drift
q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_01.description: I look for headers that change font size halfway through or bullet points that switch styles. It tells me they stopped proofreading before they were done.

q_04__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Distinguish if intuition relies on surface features (e.g., citation density) versus deep semantic markers (e.g., claim-evidence linkage) that trigger the grade.

q_04__res_L2_00_01.description: Assessing the trustworthiness of intuitive cues when translated into explicit rules.

q_04__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Quantify Cue False-Positive Rate
q_04__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Quantify the failure rate of intuitive cues to determine where novice verification is most needed before finalizing a grade.

q_04__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Evaluate Visual Encoding Methods
q_04__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Evaluate visual encoding methods (e.g., color-coding, margin icons) to make invisible quality cues visible to novices.

q_04__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Measure if cue-based scaffolding reduces the time-to-decision for novices without sacrificing grading accuracy.

q_04__res_leaf_02_00_00.description: Identify rubric criteria that fail to capture the expert's intuitive 'A-grade' signals for potential rubric redesign.
